roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 5 milestones: Aarav is a senior-level engineer with 6 years of experience, aiming for a significant leap in system design and distributed systems expertise. This requires a balanced progression (due to medium confidence) through distinct identity transitions, from foundational distributed systems to strategic architecture, aligning with the 7 hrs/week availability over 112 days.

vision_profile.current_state: A backend engineer proficient in Java/Spring Boot, executing assigned tasks, but lacking depth in system design and distributed systems for senior-level decision-making.
vision_profile.main_blocker: His biggest fear is that weak system design depth will block him in senior engineering interviews, preventing him from owning larger architectural decisions.
vision_profile.top_motivation: He is highly motivated to move into a senior backend role to own larger engineering decisions and contribute strategically to system architecture.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can design and implement robust backend services adhering to core distributed system principles, understanding trade-offs in consistency and availability.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to cover the foundational breadth required for a distributed systems architect: core concepts, advanced backend patterns, and data consistency/messaging, ensuring distinct capability clusters.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming the architect who lays the resilient groundwork for complex, distributed applications, moving beyond single-service execution.

project.title: Design & Implement a Distributed Notification Service
project.description: Design and implement a scalable, fault-tolerant notification service capable of sending messages across various channels (email, SMS, push). Focus on message queuing, idempotency, and retry mechanisms in a distributed environment.
project.deliverable: Deployed service with API documentation, system design document, and a brief on chosen consistency models.
seeded_errors: The notification service experiences duplicate messages due to incorrect idempotency key handling in a retry mechanism.

modules.title: Distributed Systems Core Concepts
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are essential to establish a strong theoretical and practical foundation in distributed systems, covering fundamental challenges and solutions.

skills.title: CAP Theorem & Consistency Models
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding CAP theorem and various consistency models (strong, eventual, causal) is crucial for making informed trade-offs in distributed system design, directly impacting reliability and performance.

video.title: CAP Theorem & Consistency Models Deep Dive

scenario.title: Resolving Data Inconsistency in a Distributed Cache

skills.title: Distributed Transactions & Idempotency
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering distributed transactions (e.g., 2PC, Saga) and idempotency is critical for ensuring data integrity and reliability in complex microservice architectures, preventing data corruption and ensuring correct state transitions.

scenario.title: Debugging a Failed Distributed Transaction in an E-commerce Checkout

skills.title: Concurrency Control & Locking
skills.why_this_skill: Effective concurrency control is fundamental for preventing race conditions and ensuring data integrity in multi-threaded and distributed applications, a core skill for senior backend engineers.

scenario.title: Diagnosing Deadlock in a High-Concurrency Payment Gateway

skills.title: Service Discovery & Load Balancing
skills.why_this_skill: Essential for managing dynamic microservice environments, enabling services to find and communicate with each other efficiently and reliably, a cornerstone of modern distributed systems.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting Service Unavailability Due to Discovery Issues

science.desc: Production debugging scenario for milestone skills

modules.title: Advanced Backend Patterns
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are necessary to equip the learner with practical patterns for building robust and maintainable distributed backend services, extending their existing Java/Spring Boot knowledge.

skills.title: Microservices Architecture & Decomposition
skills.why_this_skill: Moving from monolithic to microservice architectures is a key senior-level skill, requiring understanding of domain-driven design and effective service decomposition strategies.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Monolith into Microservices: Identifying Bounded Contexts

skills.title: API Gateway Design & Implementation
skills.why_this_skill: An API Gateway is crucial for managing external access to microservices, handling concerns like routing, authentication, and rate limiting, simplifying client interactions.

scenario.title: Optimizing API Gateway Performance for High Traffic

skills.title: Circuit Breaker & Bulkhead Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: These resilience patterns are vital for preventing cascading failures in distributed systems, improving overall system stability and fault tolerance, a must-have for senior engineers.

scenario.title: Preventing Cascading Failures in a Payment Microservice

skills.title: Event-Driven Architecture Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding event-driven architectures is key for building loosely coupled, scalable, and reactive distributed systems, enabling asynchronous communication and better fault tolerance.

scenario.title: Designing an Event-Driven Order Processing System

modules.title: Data Consistency & Messaging
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are necessary to address Aarav's gap in Kafka architecture and deepen his understanding of distributed data management and messaging patterns.

skills.title: Kafka Fundamentals & Core Concepts
skills.why_this_skill: Kafka is a critical component in modern distributed systems for high-throughput, fault-tolerant messaging. Understanding its core concepts is essential for designing event-driven architectures.

scenario.title: Optimizing Kafka Producer Throughput for High-Volume Data Ingestion

skills.title: Advanced Message Queues & Brokers
skills.why_this_skill: Beyond Kafka, understanding other message brokers (RabbitMQ, SQS) and their use cases allows for selecting the right tool for specific distributed messaging needs.

scenario.title: Designing a Reliable Asynchronous Task Processing System

skills.title: Distributed Database Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Extending beyond single-node PostgreSQL/Redis, understanding sharding, replication, and partitioning is crucial for scaling data storage in distributed systems.

scenario.title: Migrating a Monolithic Database to a Sharded Architecture

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Directly addresses Aarav's fear by preparing him for senior engineering interviews focused on distributed systems, testing his ability to apply learned concepts.

scenario.title: Designing a Global Real-time Chat Application

science.desc: Design a highly available and scalable URL shortening service, discussing consistency models and fault tolerance.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can architect, deploy, and manage backend services effectively on AWS, leveraging cloud-native patterns for scalability and resilience.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are selected to cover the breadth of cloud-native backend specialization: core AWS services, containerization, and specific cloud deployment patterns, addressing Aarav's AWS deployment gap.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming the cloud-savvy engineer who translates distributed system principles into robust, scalable, and cost-effective cloud solutions.

project.title: Build & Deploy a Serverless API with AWS Lambda & API Gateway
project.description: Develop a serverless REST API using AWS Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB. Implement authentication, data validation, and deploy using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with AWS SAM or Terraform.
project.deliverable: Deployed serverless application, IaC templates, API documentation, and a cost analysis report.
seeded_errors: The serverless API experiences unexpected cold starts and high latency due to inefficient Lambda configuration and API Gateway integration.

modules.title: AWS Core Services for Backend
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are chosen to provide a comprehensive understanding of essential AWS services for building and operating backend applications, focusing on practical application.

skills.title: AWS EC2, VPC, & ELB for Scalability
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering foundational AWS compute (EC2), networking (VPC), and load balancing (ELB) services is crucial for deploying and scaling traditional backend applications in the cloud.

scenario.title: Designing a Highly Available Web Application on AWS

skills.title: AWS RDS & DynamoDB for Data Storage
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding AWS's managed database services (relational and NoSQL) is essential for selecting the right data store for different application needs and scaling data layers efficiently.

scenario.title: Optimizing DynamoDB Performance for a High-Traffic User Profile Service

skills.title: AWS SQS, SNS, & Lambda for Serverless
skills.why_this_skill: These services form the backbone of serverless and event-driven architectures on AWS, enabling highly scalable, cost-effective, and decoupled backend systems.

scenario.title: Designing a Serverless Image Processing Pipeline

skills.title: AWS Security & IAM Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Security is paramount in cloud environments. Understanding IAM, roles, policies, and best practices is critical for building secure and compliant applications on AWS.

scenario.title: Auditing & Hardening IAM Policies for a Production Application

science.desc: Production debugging scenario for milestone skills

modules.title: Containerization & Orchestration
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are included to provide a deep understanding of containerization with Docker and orchestration with Kubernetes, essential for modern cloud-native deployments.

skills.title: Docker Containerization Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Docker is the de-facto standard for packaging applications. Mastering it is crucial for consistent deployments across development, testing, and production environments.

scenario.title: Reducing Docker Image Size and Build Times for a Spring Boot App

skills.title: Kubernetes Fundamentals & Deployments
skills.why_this_skill: Kubernetes is the leading container orchestration platform. Understanding its core concepts is vital for deploying, scaling, and managing containerized applications at scale.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Failed Kubernetes Deployment

skills.title: AWS ECS & EKS for Container Orchestration
skills.why_this_skill: Directly addresses Aarav's need for AWS deployment patterns by focusing on AWS's managed container services, enabling efficient and scalable container deployments in the cloud.
lessons: ECS Fargate vs. EC2 Launch Types
Integrating ECS/EKS with other AWS Services

scenario.title: Migrating a Legacy Application to AWS EKS

skills.title: CI/CD for Containerized Applications
skills.why_this_skill: Automating the build, test, and deployment of containerized applications is crucial for rapid and reliable releases in a cloud-native environment.

scenario.title: Implementing a CI/CD Pipeline for an EKS Microservice

modules.title: Cloud Deployment Patterns
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are included to provide a comprehensive understanding of various cloud deployment patterns, enabling Aarav to make informed architectural decisions for AWS deployments.

skills.title: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) with Terraform/CloudFormation
skills.why_this_skill: IaC is fundamental for reproducible, version-controlled, and automated cloud infrastructure provisioning, a critical skill for any senior cloud engineer.

scenario.title: Provisioning a Multi-Tier Application Environment with Terraform

skills.title: Serverless Architecture Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Deep dive into serverless patterns beyond basic Lambda functions, enabling the design of highly scalable, cost-effective, and maintenance-free backend systems.
lessons: Serverless API Design with API Gateway

scenario.title: Designing a Real-time Data Ingestion Pipeline using Kinesis and Lambda

skills.title: Hybrid & Multi-Cloud Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: For enterprise-level systems, understanding hybrid and multi-cloud approaches is critical for resilience, vendor lock-in avoidance, and compliance, a key strategic skill.

scenario.title: Designing a Disaster Recovery Strategy for a Hybrid Cloud Environment

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: Cloud Architecture
skills.why_this_skill: Prepares Aarav for interview questions specifically on cloud architecture and AWS deployment patterns, directly addressing his identified skill gap and fear.

scenario.title: Designing a Cost-Optimized, Highly Available E-commerce Platform on AWS

science.desc: Architect a real-time analytics pipeline on AWS, discussing service choices, data flow, and cost considerations.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: The learner can design and document highly scalable and performant backend systems, effectively utilizing advanced messaging and data streaming technologies.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to transform Aarav into a scalable systems designer: focusing on performance engineering, advanced Kafka architecture, and critical technical design documentation, directly addressing his core skill gaps.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming the engineer who crafts systems that effortlessly handle massive scale, ensuring peak performance and clear communication of complex designs.

project.title: Architect & Document a High-Throughput Data Ingestion System
project.description: Design and implement a system to ingest and process millions of events per second using Kafka, integrating with a scalable data store. Produce comprehensive technical design documentation for the system.
project.deliverable: Deployed ingestion pipeline, detailed system design document (HLD & LLD), and performance benchmarks.
seeded_errors: The Kafka consumer group experiences rebalancing storms and message processing delays under high load due to incorrect partition assignment and consumer configuration.

modules.title: Scalability & Performance Engineering
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are critical for designing and optimizing systems for extreme scale and performance, covering both architectural patterns and practical optimization techniques.

skills.title: Advanced System Design Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Deepening knowledge of advanced system design patterns (e.g., sharding, caching, proxies) is crucial for building highly scalable and resilient architectures, addressing Aarav's system design depth gap.

scenario.title: Scaling a Social Media Feed Service to Billions of Users

skills.title: Performance Tuning & Profiling
skills.why_this_skill: Identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks is a core responsibility of a senior engineer. This skill provides tools and techniques for optimizing application speed and resource usage.

scenario.title: Diagnosing and Resolving High Latency in a Spring Boot Microservice

skills.title: Load & Stress Testing Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Proactively identifying performance limits and bottlenecks through rigorous testing is essential for ensuring systems can handle anticipated (and unanticipated) traffic, preventing production outages.

scenario.title: Designing a Load Test Plan for a New API Gateway

skills.title: Advanced Data Partitioning & Sharding
skills.why_this_skill: Deepening the understanding of data partitioning and sharding techniques is crucial for horizontally scaling databases and managing massive datasets in distributed systems.

scenario.title: Designing a Sharding Strategy for a Global E-commerce Product Catalog

modules.title: Advanced Kafka & Stream Processing
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are dedicated to mastering Kafka architecture and stream processing, directly addressing Aarav's identified skill gap and enabling him to build real-time data pipelines.

skills.title: Kafka Architecture & Operations
skills.why_this_skill: Moving beyond fundamentals, understanding Kafka's internal architecture, cluster management, and operational best practices is crucial for deploying and maintaining high-performance Kafka systems.

scenario.title: Diagnosing High Latency in a Production Kafka Cluster

skills.title: Kafka Streams & KSQL for Real-time Processing
skills.why_this_skill: Enables building powerful, fault-tolerant real-time stream processing applications directly on Kafka, crucial for analytics, event enrichment, and reactive microservices.

video.title: Building Real-time Applications with Kafka Streams

scenario.title: Implementing a Real-time Fraud Detection System with Kafka Streams

skills.title: Data Streaming Patterns & Architectures
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding common data streaming patterns (e.g., CDC, stream-table duality) is essential for designing robust and efficient real-time data pipelines and event-driven systems.

scenario.title: Architecting a Real-time Inventory Synchronization System

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: Kafka & Streaming
skills.why_this_skill: Prepares Aarav for advanced interview questions on Kafka architecture and real-time stream processing, solidifying his expertise in a critical distributed systems component.

scenario.title: Designing a Real-time Recommendation Engine using Kafka and KSQLDB

modules.title: Technical Design Documentation & Review
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are dedicated to mastering technical design documentation and review processes, directly addressing Aarav's identified skill gap and enabling him to lead design discussions effectively.

skills.title: High-Level & Low-Level Design Documentation
skills.why_this_skill: Producing clear and comprehensive HLD/LLD documents is a critical senior-level skill for communicating architectural decisions, aligning teams, and ensuring successful project execution.

scenario.title: Writing an HLD for a New User Authentication Service

skills.title: Architectural Decision Records (ADRs)
skills.why_this_skill: ADRs are crucial for documenting significant architectural decisions, their context, and consequences, providing clarity and historical context for future development and maintenance.

scenario.title: Documenting the Decision to Adopt a New Database Technology

skills.title: Design Review & Feedback Facilitation
skills.why_this_skill: Leading and participating in design reviews is a key aspect of senior engineering, ensuring architectural soundness, identifying flaws early, and fostering collaborative decision-making.

scenario.title: Facilitating a Design Review for a Critical Microservice Migration

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: System Design Depth
skills.why_this_skill: Directly addresses Aarav's biggest fear by providing targeted interview preparation for complex system design questions, requiring him to articulate and justify architectural decisions.
lessons: Mock Interview Practice: Full System Design

scenario.title: Designing a Distributed Payment Processing System with High Availability and Low Latency

science.desc: Design a global ride-sharing platform like Uber, focusing on matching, real-time tracking, and surge pricing mechanisms.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: The learner can lead the design and implementation of highly resilient, fault-tolerant, and observable distributed systems, ensuring operational excellence and rapid incident resolution.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are selected to transform Aarav into a resilient and observable systems lead: focusing on reliability engineering, advanced observability, and operational incident management, crucial for senior leadership.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming the guardian of system stability, proactively building fault-tolerant architectures and ensuring clear visibility into every component's health.

project.title: Build an Observability Stack for a Microservice Ecosystem
project.description: Implement a comprehensive observability solution (logging, metrics, tracing) for a set of interconnected microservices. Configure alerts and dashboards to proactively detect and diagnose issues.
project.deliverable: Deployed observability stack, configured dashboards and alerts, and a post-mortem analysis of a simulated incident using collected data.
seeded_errors: The distributed tracing system fails to correlate requests across service boundaries due to incorrect header propagation, making root cause analysis difficult.

modules.title: Reliability & Fault Tolerance
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are essential for designing systems that can withstand failures and maintain high availability, covering advanced resilience patterns and disaster recovery.

skills.title: Chaos Engineering & Resilience Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Proactively injecting failures into systems (Chaos Engineering) is a cutting-edge practice for identifying weaknesses before they cause production outages, a critical skill for building truly resilient systems.

video.title: Building Resilient Systems with Chaos Engineering

scenario.title: Conducting a Chaos Experiment to Test Database Failure Resilience

skills.title: Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity
skills.why_this_skill: Designing and implementing robust disaster recovery plans is paramount for minimizing downtime and data loss during catastrophic events, ensuring business continuity.

scenario.title: Developing a Disaster Recovery Plan for a Critical Financial Service

skills.title: Data Backup & Recovery Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring data integrity and availability through robust backup and recovery mechanisms is a foundational aspect of system reliability, preventing data loss and enabling quick restoration.

scenario.title: Restoring a Production Database After Accidental Data Deletion

skills.title: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Adopting SRE principles shifts focus from reactive operations to proactive engineering for reliability, a mindset crucial for senior roles in high-scale environments.

scenario.title: Defining SLOs and Error Budgets for a Critical API Service

modules.title: Observability & Monitoring
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are crucial for building comprehensive observability into distributed systems, enabling proactive issue detection and rapid root cause analysis.

skills.title: Distributed Logging & Centralized Aggregation
skills.why_this_skill: Effective logging and centralized aggregation (ELK, Splunk) are fundamental for debugging and monitoring distributed systems, providing insights into application behavior across services.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Production Issue Using Centralized Logs

skills.title: Distributed Tracing with OpenTelemetry/Jaeger
skills.why_this_skill: Distributed tracing is indispensable for understanding request flows across microservices, identifying latency bottlenecks, and debugging complex interactions in distributed systems.

scenario.title: Identifying a Latency Spike Root Cause Using Distributed Traces

skills.title: Metrics, Monitoring & Alerting
skills.why_this_skill: Defining key metrics, setting up robust monitoring, and configuring effective alerts are essential for proactive system health management and rapid response to issues.

scenario.title: Configuring Alerts for Critical Service Health Degradation

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: Observability & SRE
skills.why_this_skill: Prepares Aarav for interview questions on observability, monitoring, and SRE practices, demonstrating his ability to ensure operational excellence and system reliability.

scenario.title: Designing an Observability Strategy for a New Microservice

modules.title: Incident Management & Post-mortems
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are included to equip Aarav with the necessary knowledge and processes for effective incident response and learning from failures, a key aspect of senior operational leadership.

skills.title: Incident Response & Management Process
skills.why_this_skill: Leading incident response is a critical senior-level responsibility. Understanding the process ensures quick resolution, minimal impact, and effective communication during outages.

scenario.title: Leading an Incident Response for a Major Production Outage

skills.title: Blameless Post-mortems & Root Cause Analysis
skills.why_this_skill: Conducting blameless post-mortems is crucial for fostering a culture of learning from failures, identifying systemic weaknesses, and preventing recurrence without assigning blame.

scenario.title: Facilitating a Post-mortem for a Customer-Impacting Service Degradation

skills.why_this_skill: Managing on-call rotations and responding effectively to alerts is a core operational responsibility. This skill covers tools and best practices for sustainable on-call duties.

scenario.title: Optimizing an On-Call Rotation to Reduce Burnout and Improve Response Times

skills.title: Senior Backend Interview: Operational Excellence
skills.why_this_skill: Prepares Aarav for interview questions on incident management, post-mortems, and operational best practices, demonstrating his readiness to lead and maintain highly reliable systems.

scenario.title: Responding to a Critical Database Failure in a Production Environment

science.desc: Describe your approach to incident management for a critical service outage, including communication, resolution, and post-mortem processes.

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: The learner can strategically lead complex system design initiatives, make high-level architectural decisions, and drive technical vision across multiple teams.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to prepare Aarav for a principal architect role: focusing on strategic system design, cross-functional technical leadership, and advanced distributed consensus, enabling him to drive technical vision.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming the visionary architect, shaping the future of complex systems and leading engineering excellence across the organization.

project.title: Define & Architect a New Core Platform Service
project.description: Lead the end-to-end architectural definition for a new, critical platform service. This includes gathering requirements, evaluating technical options, designing the system (HLD & LLD), and presenting the solution to stakeholders.
project.deliverable: Comprehensive architectural proposal, detailed system design documents, and a presentation deck for executive review.
seeded_errors: The proposed architecture for the new platform service fails to adequately address cross-region data consistency requirements, leading to potential data integrity issues.

skills.why_this_skill: Leading architectural evolution and strategic refactoring is key for maintaining system health and adapting to changing business needs, a core responsibility of a principal architect.
